title: Fibonacci Series

content.problemStatement:
Given an integer NNN, output the NthN^{th}Nth term in the Fibonacci Series.

The Fibonacci Series is: `0 1 1 2 3 5 8...`

Complete the function `fib()` function to solve this problem using **recursion**.


Input Format

The input contains a single integer NNN.


Output Format
Output the NthN^{th}Nth term in the Fibonacci Series.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the Nth term of the Fibonacci series on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is one test case. N=1 corresponds to the first element of the series, which is 0.
One test case with N=2. The second element of the Fibonacci series is 1.
One test case with N=7. The series up to the 7th term is 0,1,1,2,3,5,8; thus the 7th term is 8.

solution.approach: The Fibonacci sequence can be defined recursively: F(1)=0, F(2)=1, and for n>2, F(n)=F(n-1)+F(n-2). Direct recursion recomputes the same values many times, leading to exponential time. By storing already computed values (memoization) in an array of size 31 we ensure each value is computed once, giving linear time per test case. The memo array is initialized with -1 and the recursive function returns the stored result when available. This fits the problem constraints (N≤30) easily.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case (N≤30), overall O(T·N).

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{static long[] dp=new long[31];static long fib(int n){if(n==1)return 0;if(n==2)return 1;if(dp[n]!=-1)return dp[n];dp[n]=fib(n-1)+fib(n-2);return dp[n];}public static void main(String[] args)throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());Arrays.fill(dp,-1);StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();for(int i=0;i<T;i++){int n=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());sb.append(fib(n)).append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # i: 0 to 20 (inclusive). Generate the same inputs as listed in hiddenTestCases.
    cases = {
        0: lambda: (print(1), print(1)),
        1: lambda: (print(1), print(2)),
        2: lambda: (print(1), print(3)),
        3: lambda: (print(1), print(30)),
        4: lambda: (print(10), [print(30) for _ in range(10)]),
        5: lambda: (print(10), [print(j) for j in range(1,11)]),
        6: lambda: (print(5), [print(25) for _ in range(5)]),
        7: lambda: (print(3), print(29), print(30), print(28)),
        8: lambda: (print(7), [print(5) for _ in range(7)]),
        9: lambda: (print(5), [print(1) for _ in range(5)]),
        10: lambda: (print(5), [print(j) for j in range(1,6)]),
        11: lambda: (print(5), [print(j) for j in [5,4,3,2,1]]),
        12: lambda: (print(2), print(13), print(13)),
        13: lambda: (print(1), print(30)),
        14: lambda: (print(1), print(1)),
        15: lambda: (print(3), [print(27) for _ in range(3)]),
        16: lambda: (print(10), [print(x) for x in [1,5,10,15,20,25,30,2,3,4]]),
        17: lambda: (print(10), [print(x) for x in [6,12,18,24,30,5,11,17,23,29]]),
        18: lambda: (print(9), [print(x) for x in [1,30,1,30,1,30,1,30,1]]),
        19: lambda: (print(4), [print(x) for x in [30,29,28,27]]),
        20: lambda: None
    }
    func = cases.get(i)
    if func:
        result = func()
        # If the lambda returns a list of prints, they have already been executed.
        # Ensure the function finishes without returning anything.
        return
